What do I do like consciously to get myself to do the things I know I have to do each day, even when I don't feel like doing them? What is there a process I use? I think I do it just randomly. Is it just so instilled in me that I just do it automatically? And the answer is no. There's a lot of time when there's things I have to get done. I don't like doing them, the things I don't enjoy yet. I managed to get myself to do them anyway and I will tell you my strategy. Now to give you some just overarching concepts here, the first one is that in general, motivation is based on two principles that people essentially move away from things that are painful. They propel themselves away from pain, right? And they get pulled towards things that give them pleasure. So some people like myself believe that I'm very pain based as a motivator. If I need to get myself off the mark and I find myself just not really being inspired and motivated in any given day, I actually will imagine all the terrible things. What's the worst case seen ever? What's going to happen to me if I don't get this done? How's it going to impact my life negatively? And I actually insinuate myself into my greatest fears. I imagine my greatest fears happening, my business is going to suffer, I'm going to not be loved by people, I'm not going to be vital anymore, I'm going to be broke, I'll be living in the fucking strategy. I future pace out all these negative things happening and I link that to not taking action. And also part of my strategy too is to imagine the pleasurable things that happen to us. Say, wow, and if I do this, I'm going to feel great, I'll get this accomplished and I'm setting the stage for this part of my vision to happen. So I always go both ways, when I am a pain more pain based for the real stuff and I really can't get it going, I focus on pain. All right, that's me. Every person is different. And then when you're having trouble, motivating yourself consciously focus on, and if I do this, I'm going to get what I want. And also for those of you on the negative side, figure out what is the negative things, what are the things that really are your away values, what you want to move away from and use those to motivate yourself. At the end of the day, we all use both, but the dominant one is the one that gets the job done. All right. And that's your motivation for the day. I love you all. We'll talk again tomorrow.
